title: Lacis ruppioides

intro: Lacis ruppioides, also known as Ruppia maritima or Lacis ruppioides, is a type of submerged aquatic plant commonly found in shallow waters and estuaries. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Lacis ruppioides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to be responsible for its therapeutic properties.
